Title: Notification service passes Customer.id instead of User.id — all notifications silently fail
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Sign in as customer (jane.kamau@example.com / Customer123!). 2. POST /api/orders with valid body including deliveryAddress/slotId/deliveryDate/quantity/paymentMethod. 3. Check: SELECT count(*) FROM "Notification"; 4. Check server logs.
Expected Result: Two Notification records created in DB (event: order.placed and event: otp.sent) per §20.1 and §15.1 of business-rules.md.
Actual Result: Zero notifications created. Server log shows: [notification-service] Failed to send notification: PrismaClientKnownRequestError: Foreign key constraint violated on the constraint: Notification_userId_fkey at lib/notifications/notification-service.ts:51
Evidence: SELECT count(*) FROM "Notification"; → 0 rows after 5 orders placed. Server log: Invalid prisma.notification.create() invocation: Foreign key constraint violated on Notification_userId_fkey at notification-service.ts:51
Root Cause: In app/api/orders/route.ts the notification calls pass customer.id (Customer PK) as customerId. The service forwards this as userId to db.notification.create(). But Notification.userId is a FK to User.id — a different ID. Every insert fails with a FK violation. The .catch(console.error) pattern silences the error.
Recommended Fix: In app/api/orders/route.ts change customer.id to customer.userId in all notification calls. Update notification-service.ts parameter naming to reflect User.id expectation.

Title: Slot capacity (SlotCapacity.bookedOrders) never incremented — overbooking undetectable
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Note an active slot ID. 2. Place 5+ orders for the same slot/band/date via any customer session. 3. Run: SELECT "bookedOrders" FROM "SlotCapacity" GROUP BY "bookedOrders";
Expected Result: Per §3.4: bookedOrders increments on order placement per zone/slot/date. Orders exceeding maxOrders (default 20) are rejected or redirected to next slot.
Actual Result: bookedOrders = 0 for all 12 SlotCapacity rows after 8 orders placed. No capacity enforcement occurs.
Evidence: SELECT "bookedOrders", count(*) FROM "SlotCapacity" GROUP BY "bookedOrders"; → bookedOrders | count → 0 | 12. Discrepancy query shows actual_orders vs capacity_booked mismatch on 3 rows.
Root Cause: lib/slots/slot-validator.ts implements full capacity logic (checkSlotAvailability; suggestNextSlot) but app/api/orders/route.ts only checks slot.isActive. The validator is never imported or called. SlotCapacity is never read or written during order placement.
Files Involved: app/api/orders/route.ts (slot check only tests isActive); lib/slots/slot-validator.ts (fully implemented; never called)
Recommended Fix: Wire lib/slots/slot-validator.ts into POST /api/orders. On order creation increment SlotCapacity.bookedOrders. On cancellation decrement it. Return 400 with next-slot suggestion when at capacity.

Title: Unauthenticated API requests return 307 HTML redirect instead of 401 JSON
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Without any session cookie call: curl -sv http://localhost:3000/api/reports?type=daily-sales. 2. Observe HTTP response code and Location header.
Expected Result: HTTP 401 with body: {"error":"Unauthorized"} so API clients can handle authentication failure programmatically.
Actual Result: HTTP 307 Temporary Redirect with Location: /login?callbackUrl=... Body is empty. Non-browser clients receive an HTML redirect they cannot parse.
Evidence: curl -sv http://localhost:3000/api/reports 2>&1 | grep "< HTTP\|< location" → HTTP/1.1 307 Temporary Redirect → location: /login?type=daily-sales&callbackUrl=http://localhost:3000/api/reports. Confirmed for 8 different API endpoints.
Root Cause: middleware.ts applies NextAuth authorized() callback to all routes including /api/*. Unauthenticated requests to API routes are redirected to /login the same as page routes. No special handling for API paths.
Files Involved: middleware.ts (uniform redirect to all routes); auth.config.ts (authorized callback — no /api/ path distinction)
Recommended Fix: In auth.config.ts authorized callback detect API routes by checking pathname.startsWith('/api/'). For API paths return NextResponse.json({error:'Unauthorized'},{status:401}) instead of the NextAuth signIn redirect.

Title: orderRef sequence uses db.order.count() — race condition causes 500 under concurrent requests
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Send two simultaneous POST /api/orders requests from the same or different customer sessions. 2. Observe that one request returns HTTP 500. 3. Confirm Order.orderRef @unique constraint is in place.
Expected Result: Each order receives a unique orderRef. Concurrent requests both succeed with distinct refs.
Actual Result: Two concurrent requests read the same count N and both attempt to insert with orderRef RAX-YYYYMMDD-00N. Second insert fails with unique constraint violation. API returns HTTP 500. Customer order is lost.
Evidence: Schema: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX Order_orderRef_key ON public.Order USING btree (orderRef). Code in app/api/orders/route.ts: const orderCount = await db.order.count(); const orderRef = generateOrderRef(orderCount + 1); — not atomic.
Root Cause: Non-atomic count-then-insert pattern. No lock; no DB sequence; no retry. Unique constraint is the only safeguard and it surfaces as an unhandled 500 rather than a clean error.
Recommended Fix: Replace count-based sequence with collision-resistant approach: append random suffix and retry on P2002; or use a PostgreSQL sequence; or catch P2002 in the catch block and return 422 instead of 500.

Title: Same-day delivery cutoff and 30-minute lead-time rules not enforced at checkout
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Sign in as any customer. 2. At 23:00 POST /api/orders with slotId = the 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM slot and deliveryDate = today. 3. Observe HTTP 201.
Expected Result: Per §3.3 minimum 30-minute lead time enforced. Per §2.2-2.3 same-day cutoff rules applied per band. A past-time slot for today should be rejected with next-slot suggestion.
Actual Result: Order created successfully for a slot that ended 11 hours ago. No time-based validation occurs. Orders placed after ORDER_CUTOFF (20:00) for same-day slots also succeed.
Evidence: Orders RAX-20260415-005 through 008 were placed at ~20:10 EAT for deliveryDate 2026-04-20 using the 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM slot. All accepted. lib/slots/slot-validator.ts exports meetsLeadTime() and checkSameDayEligibility() — neither imported from app/api/orders/route.ts.
Root Cause: Same root cause as BUG-002. lib/slots/slot-validator.ts time-based validation functions are fully implemented but never called from the order placement route.
Files Involved: app/api/orders/route.ts (no time-based validation); lib/slots/slot-validator.ts (meetsLeadTime; checkSameDayEligibility — never called)
Recommended Fix: Include same-day cutoff and lead-time checks when wiring in the slot validator for BUG-002. Call checkSlotAvailability() which internally applies both time and capacity rules.

Title: Seeded loyalty counts do not match qualifying completed orders in the database
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run reconciliation query: SELECT u.name; c.loyaltyCount as stored; COUNT(o.id) as actual_qualifying FROM Customer c JOIN User u ON u.id=c.userId LEFT JOIN Order o ON o.customerId=c.id AND o.status='COMPLETED' AND o.countsForLoyalty=true AND o.isLoyaltyReward=false GROUP BY u.name; c.loyaltyCount;
Expected Result: loyaltyCount matches count of completed qualifying orders in DB per §11.1.
Actual Result: John Njoroge: stored=3; actual=0. Jane Kamau: stored=7; actual=1. Discrepancy of 6 orders for jane; 3 for john.
Evidence: name | stored | actual_qualifying → John Njoroge | 3 | 0 → Jane Kamau | 7 | 1 → Mary Achieng | 0 | 0. Jane's count correctly increments at runtime (tested; verified) — the gap is in seed data only.
Root Cause: prisma/seed.ts pre-sets loyaltyCount=7 (jane) and loyaltyCount=3 (john) to simulate off-platform history but does not seed corresponding COMPLETED Order rows. Runtime increment logic is correct.
Files Involved: prisma/seed.ts (loyaltyCount: 7 and loyaltyCount: 3 set without matching COMPLETED Order rows)
Recommended Fix: Seed additional COMPLETED qualifying Order rows for jane.kamau (6 more) and john.njoroge (3 more) to make the stored counts consistent with actual DB state; or add a seed comment documenting the intentional divergence.

Title: Provisional pricing on address-only Band 2 orders not surfaced to customer in API response
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Sign in as any customer. 2. POST /api/orders with deliveryAddress: Kahawa Sukari Estate and no deliveryLat/deliveryLng. 3. Observe API response. 4. Check: SELECT isProvisionalPrice FROM Order WHERE orderRef='<ref>';
Expected Result: Per §7.6 customer shall see provisional delivery charges when location is ambiguous. API response should include an isProvisional flag.
Actual Result: API returns only {id; orderRef}. isProvisionalPrice=true is stored in DB but not returned. Customer receives no indication their price may change.
Evidence: POST /api/orders response: {"id":"cmo0hkamx000frfnn7heuxcnu";"orderRef":"RAX-20260415-006"}. SELECT isProvisionalPrice FROM Order WHERE orderRef='RAX-20260415-006'; → t. Order detail page shows no provisional warning.
Root Cause: app/api/orders/route.ts returns only {id; orderRef} on success. The isProvisionalPrice flag is persisted but excluded from the response. Customer order detail page has no conditional rendering for provisional state.
Files Involved: app/api/orders/route.ts (response body — only id and orderRef returned); app/(customer)/customer/orders/[id]/page.tsx (no provisional pricing alert rendered)
Recommended Fix: Include isProvisional: order.isProvisionalPrice in the 201 response. Render a visible alert on the customer order detail page when isProvisionalPrice is true.

Title: lib/auth/rbac.ts is dead code — never imported anywhere
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run: grep -rn "from.*rbac" app/ lib/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx". 2. Observe zero results.
Expected Result: All files in lib/ are actively used. Dead code is absent.
Actual Result: lib/auth/rbac.ts exists and contains role-check logic but is never imported by any file. Zero grep matches across the entire codebase.
Evidence: grep -rn "from.*rbac" app/ lib/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" → (no output; 0 matches). File confirmed present: lib/auth/rbac.ts
Root Cause: lib/auth/rbac.ts was scaffolded in Phase 1 but superseded by lib/auth/api-auth.ts (API routes) and lib/auth/session.ts (page layouts). The original file was never removed.
Recommended Fix: Delete lib/auth/rbac.ts. Verify that no functionality it contains is absent from api-auth.ts or session.ts before deleting.
